action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 2 failed with segmentation fault on torch_geometric import, indicating version incompatibility between torch and the pre-installed PyG dependencies. Analysis: The pre-installed torch-geometric and related libs (scatter, sparse, etc.) don't match the torch/cuda version in the environment, causing crash. Dynamic install using the correct wheel URL from pyg.org will resolve it. The model, dataset, and predict scripts look correct for a basic batched GCN baseline. Decision: Run a command to uninstall old PyG libs and reinstall matching versions, test imports, then execute train.py (5 epochs), and submit_eval on the best checkpoint to get baseline MAE and confirm pipeline works. This will also give us time measurement for future planning.
action.command:
pip uninstall -y torch-scatter torch-sparse torch-cluster torch-spline-conv torch-geometric 2>/dev/null || true && python -c '
import torch
import subprocess as sp
torch_ver = torch.__version__.split("+")[0]
cuda_ver = "cpu" if not torch.cuda.is_available() else "cu" + torch.version.cuda.replace(".", "")
print(f"Installing for torch {torch_ver} + {cuda_ver}")
url = f"https://data.pyg.org/whl/torch-{torch_ver}+{cuda_ver}.html"
pkgs = ["torch-scatter", "torch-sparse", "torch-cluster", "torch-spline-conv"]
for pkg in pkgs:
    sp.check_call(["pip", "install", "--no-cache-dir", "-q", pkg, "-f", url])
sp.check_call(["pip", "install", "--no-cache-dir", "-q", "torch-geometric"])
print("PyG installation completed successfully")
' && python -c '
import torch
import torch_geometric as pyg
print("Torch:", torch.__version__)
print("CUDA available:", torch.cuda.is_available())
print("PyG:", pyg.__version__)
print("Successfully imported all packages!")
' && python train.py
